documentation.value: Writes objects to stdout, each followed by a newline. Calls to_s on non-string objects.
One or more objects to print. Arrays are printed element-per-line.
Writes objects to stdout without adding a newline. Sets $_ to the last argument.
One or more objects to print.
Prints the inspect form of each object followed by a newline. Useful for debugging.
One or more objects to inspect and print.
Reads a line from standard input. Returns nil at end of file.
The line separator. Default is $/ (newline).
If true, removes the trailing separator from the result.
Returns a new string with the trailing record separator removed.
The separator to remove. Default is $/ (newline). Empty string removes all trailing newlines.
Divides a string into substrings based on a delimiter pattern.
The delimiter: a String, Regexp, or nil (splits on whitespace). ' ' splits on runs of whitespace.
Maximum number of fields. Negative values preserve trailing empty strings.
Returns a string created by joining all elements with the given separator.
The string to place between elements. Default is $, (usually nil).
Appends one or more objects to the end of the array. Returns the array itself.
One or more objects to append.
Removes and returns the last element, or the last n elements as an array.
Optional. Number of elements to pop. Returns an array when specified.
Returns a new array with the results of running the block once for every element.
A block that receives each element and returns the transformed value.
Returns a new array containing all elements for which the block returns a truthy value.
A block that receives each element and returns true to include it.

signatures.label: Enumerable#reduce(initial = nil, sym = nil) { |memo, obj| block } -> obj

documentation.value: Combines all elements by applying a binary operation (block or named method).
Optional initial value for the accumulator.
A block receiving the accumulator and each element, returning the new accumulator value.
Calls the given block once for each element, passing each element as a parameter.
A block to execute for each element.
Returns the number of elements in the array. Alias: size.
Returns true if the string contains the given substring.
The substring to search for.
Loads the given file or gem. Returns true if loaded for the first time, false if already loaded.
The name of the file or gem to require (e.g., 'json', 'net/http', './my_file').
Opens a file. With a block, the file is automatically closed when the block terminates.
The path to the file.
The open mode: 'r' (read), 'w' (write), 'a' (append), 'r+' (read-write), 'b' (binary).
Optional block receiving the file object. File is closed automatically after block.
Parses a JSON string and returns a Ruby data structure (Hash, Array, String, etc.).
The JSON string to parse.
Options hash. symbolize_names: true converts keys to symbols.
